action.hypothesis: Keeping `lowrank_two_stage_residual` fixed and dropping only `K` from 2 to 1 will test the endpoint of this family's ultra-low-K tail and determine whether it can still produce a non-dominated frontier point at the minimum support budget.
action.summary: Continue the controlled same-family sweep with a parameter-only run at `K=1`, while pinning the established recipe (`ARCHITECTURE=lowrank_two_stage_residual`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`). The `K=2` run remained keep-worthy, so the most informative next step is to finish the low-K trace before switching to routed variants or other families.
action.self_review: This preserves strict variable isolation, completes the current low-K sweep, and answers the highest-value open question on the present Pareto lane before spending effort on a new architecture.
notes_to_memory: Round 7 extends the controlled `lowrank_two_stage_residual` K sweep from 2 to 1 with all other recipe fields pinned.
The family has remained keep-worthy from K=32 down through K=2, so testing `K=1` is the cleanest way to locate the endpoint of its Pareto-relevant low-K tail before introducing architecture changes.
next_hypotheses: If `K=1` stays competitive, switch focus to a routed variant around K=16-24 to see whether support allocation can bend the mid-K frontier further.
If `K=1` degrades sharply, shift back to the 16-24 regime and test a routed variant for better support allocation.
If `K=1` is borderline but interesting, keep the family fixed and tune stage allocation rather than changing the broader recipe.

result.description: Continue the controlled same-family sweep with a parameter-only run at `K=1`, while pinning the established recipe (`ARCHITECTURE=lowrank_two_stage_residual`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`). The `K=2` run remained keep-worthy, so the most informative next step is to finish the low-K trace before switching to routed variants or other families.
result.self_review: This preserves strict variable isolation, completes the current low-K sweep, and answers the highest-value open question on the present Pareto lane before spending effort on a new architecture.
